1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: O YOU ENFOLDED in your mantle (of reform),

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: O the Cloaked One! (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him)

1.author: Arberry
1.text: O thou shrouded in thy mantle,

1.author: Maududi
1.text: O you enveloped in your cloak!

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: O thou enveloped in thy cloak,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: O you wrapped up in your mantle!

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: O you who covers himself [with a garment],

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: O, you, wrapped in your cloak,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: O thou wrapped up (in the mantle)!

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And proclaim the Purity of your Lord.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: and magnify the glory of your Lord,

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: proclaim the greatness of your Lord,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: Proclaim the glory of your Lord;

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: And thy Lord do thou magnify!

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: And thy garments keep free from stain!

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And keep away from Ar-Rujz (the idols)!

5.author: Qarai
5.text: and keep away from all impurity!

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Do not bestow favours in expectation of return,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: And do not favour others in order to receive more.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Give not, thinking to gain greater

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: And bestow not favour that thou mayest receive more.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: And give not a thing in order to have more (or consider not your deeds of Allah's obedience as a favour to Allah).

6.author: Itani
6.text: And show no favor seeking gain.

6.author: Maududi
6.text: and bestow not favour in order to seek from others a greater return,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: And give not a thing in order to have more.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: And show not favour, seeking wordly gain!

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Do not grant a favour seeking a greater gain,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Do not give, thinking to gain greater

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: And do not confer favor to acquire more

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: and do not think that by doing such deeds, you have done a great favor to God.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: And bestow not favors that you may receive again with increase,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: do not bestow a favour in the expectation of receiving more in return;

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Nor expect, in giving, any increase (for thyself)!

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: And persevere in the way of your Lord.

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And for the sake of your Lord, patiently endure.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: and be patient unto thy Lord.

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And for the good-will of thy Lord be thou patient.

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And be patient for the sake of your Lord (i.e. perform your duty to Allah)!

7.author: Itani
7.text: And be constant for your Lord.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: and persevere for your Lord's sake.

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And be patient for the sake of your Lord!

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: For the sake of thy Lord, be patient!

7.author: Qarai
7.text: and be patient for the sake of your Lord.

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: But for your Lord be patient.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: Exercise patience to please your Lord.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And for the sake of your Lord, be patient.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: and for the sake of your Lord, be patient.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: But, for thy Lord's (Cause), be patient and constant!

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: So when the Trumpet will be blown.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: For when the Trump is sounded

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Then when the horn shall be blown,

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Then, when the Trumpet is sounded (i.e. its second blowing);

8.author: Maududi
8.text: When the Trumpet shall be sounded,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Then, when the Naqur is sounded.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: For when the trumpet shall sound,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: When the Trumpet will be sounded,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: And when the trumpet is blown,

8.author: Shakir
8.text: For when the trumpet is sounded,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Finally, when the Trumpet is sounded,

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: It will be a day of distress,

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: So that is a tough day.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: that day will be a harsh day,

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: That shall be - that Day-a day hard.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Truly, that Day will be a Hard Day.

9.author: Itani
9.text: That Day will be a difficult day.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: that will surely be a hard day,

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Truly, that Day will be a Hard Day.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Surely that day will be a day of anguish,

9.author: Qarai
9.text: that will be a day of hardship,

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: that shall be a Harsh Day

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: That Day will be a difficult day

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: it will be a hard day

9.author: Shakir
9.text: That, at that time, shall be a difficult day,

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: that Day will be a hard and distressing Day.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: That will be- that Day - a Day of Distress,-

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Far from easy for the disbelievers.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: not an easy day for the unbelievers.

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Far from easy for the disbelievers.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: not at all easy for the faithless.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: and it will not be easy for the unbelievers.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: and for the disbelievers, in particular, it will not be at all easy.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: For the unbelievers, anything but easy.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: It will not be easy for those who deny the truth.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Far from easy for those without Faith.

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Leave him to Me whom I created alone,

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: Leave him to Me, the one whom I created single.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Leave Me with him whom I created alone,

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Let Me alone with him whom I created lonely.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Leave Me Alone (to deal) with whom I created Alone (without any means, i.e. Al-Walid bin Al-Mughirah Al-Makhzumi)!

11.author: Itani
11.text: Leave Me to him whom I created alone.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Leave Me with him whom I alone have created,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Leave Me alone (to deal) with whom I created lonely.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Leave Me (to deal) with him whom I created lonely,

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Leave Me [to deal] with him whom I created alone,

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Leave Me alone with he whom I created

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Leave Me with the one I created alone

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: Leave to Me the one, whom I have created all by Myself,

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Leave Me and him whom I created alone,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Leave Me alone [to deal] with him whom I have created alone,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Leave Me alone, (to deal) with the (creature) whom I created (bare and) alone!-

12.author: Arberry
12.text: and appointed for him ample wealth

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And for whom I appointed wealth extended.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And then granted him resources in abundance.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: whom I have endowed with abundant riches,

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And then granted him resources in abundance.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And then bestowed upon him ample means,

12.author: Qarai
12.text: and furnished him with extensive means,

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: and designated for him ample wealth,

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And to whom I granted extensive wealth

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: and whom I have granted abundant wealth

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: and to whom I have granted resources in abundance,

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: To whom I granted resources in abundance,

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And sons always present by his side,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And gave him sons present before him.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And sons present by his side.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And children to be by his side!

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and sons ever present with him,

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And sons abiding in his presence

13.author: Qarai
13.text: [gave him] sons to be at his side,

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: and sons that stand in witness.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: and children living in his presence,

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And sons dwelling in his presence,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and sons to be by his side,

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And sons to be by his side!-

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And made things easy for them.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And made several preparations for him.

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and made all things smooth for him;

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And for whom I smoothed everything.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And made life smooth and comfortable for him!

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and for whom I have smoothed the way (to power and riches),

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And made life smooth and comfortable for him.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: I made things smooth and easy for him,

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And spread [everything] before him, easing [his life].

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: whose life I have made run smoothly

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And I adjusted affairs for him adjustably;

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and whom I have provided with every resource,

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: To whom I made (life) smooth and comfortable!

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Yet he wants that I should give him more.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Yet he desires that I should give more.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: then he is eager that I should do more.

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: And who yet coveteth that shall increase.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: After all that he desires that I should give more;

15.author: Itani
15.text: Then he wants Me to add yet more!

15.author: Maududi
15.text: and who still greedily desires that I should bestow upon him more.

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: After all that he desires that I should give more.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Yet he desireth that I should give more.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Still he is eager that I should give him more.

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: yet he is eager that I increase him.

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Then he desires that I should add more.

15.author: Shakir
15.text: And yet he desires that I should add more!

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: and yet, he greedily desires that I give him even more!

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Yet is he greedy-that I should add (yet more);-

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: Never. He is refractory of Our signs.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: Never! For he is an enemy to Our signs!

16.author: Arberry
16.text: Nay! He is forward unto Our signs;

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: By no means! Verily he hath been Unto Our signs a foe.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Nay! Verily, he has been stubborn and opposing Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.).

16.author: Itani
16.text: By no means! He was stubborn towards Our revelations.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: By no means; he is stubbornly opposed to Our Signs.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: Nay! Verily, he has been opposing Our Ayat.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Nay! For lo! he hath been stubborn to Our revelations.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: No indeed! He is an obstinate opponent of Our signs.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Not at all! Surely, he has been disobedient to Our verses.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: No! Indeed, he has been toward Our verses obstinate.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Never will he receive more. He has been hostile to Our revelations.

16.author: Shakir
16.text: By no means! surely he offers opposition to Our communications.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: By no means! He has been stubbornly hostile to Our revelation:

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: By no means! For to Our Signs he has been refractory!

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: I shall inflict on him hardship,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: Soon I shall mount him on Saood, the hill of fire.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: and I shall constrain him to a hard ascent.

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Anon I shall afflict him with a fearful woe.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: I shall oblige him to (climb a slippery mountain in the Hell-fire called As-Sa'ud, or to) face a severe torment!

17.author: Maududi
17.text: I shall soon constrain him to a hard ascent.

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: I shall force him to Sa`ud!

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: On him I shall impose a fearful doom.

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Soon I will overwhelm him with hardship.

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: I will constrain him to a hard ascent.

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: I will cover him with arduous torment.

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: We shall make him suffer the torment of hell without relief.

17.author: Shakir
17.text: I will make a distressing punishment overtake him.

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: I shall force him to endure a painful uphill climb!

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Soon will I visit him with a mount of calamities!

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: For he had thought and calculated.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: Indeed he thought, and inwardly decided.

18.author: Maududi
18.text: He reflected and then hatched a scheme.

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: For lo! he did consider; then he planned -

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: For he thought and he plotted --

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: For he thought and he plotted;-

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: May he be accursed, how he planned!

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: So accursed be he, how evilly did he decide!

19.author: Arberry
19.text: death seize him, how he determined!

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: Perish he! How moliciously he devised!

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: So let him be cursed! How he plotted!

19.author: Itani
19.text: May he perish, how he analyzed.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Ruin seize him, how did he hatch a scheme?

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: So let him be cursed, how he plotted!

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: death seized him, how was his determining!

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: So may he be destroyed [for] how he deliberated.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: But may he be cursed how he plotted;

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: and woe to him; how he plotted!

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: And woe to him! How he plotted!-

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: May be then be accursed, how he plotted!

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Again accursed be he, how evilly did he decide!

20.author: Arberry
20.text: Again, death seize him, how he determined!

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And again perish he! How maliciously he devised!

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And once more let him be cursed, how he plotted!

20.author: Itani
20.text: Again: may he perish, how he analyzed.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: Again, ruin seize him, how did he hatch a scheme?

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And once more let him be cursed, how he plotted!

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: Again (self-)destroyed is he, how he planned! -

20.author: Qarai
20.text: Again, perish he, how he decided!

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: Again, death seized him, how was his determining!

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: Then may he be destroyed [for] how he deliberated.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: What an evil plan he has made!

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Again, may he be cursed how he plotted;

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: Let him be destroyed. How he calculated!

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: Yea, Woe to him; How he plotted!-

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: He then dared to lift his gaze.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: May he be condemned again for his schemes! He looked around,

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: And frowned and puckered his brow,

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: Then he frowned and he looked in a bad tempered way;

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Then he frowned and he looked in a bad tempered way;

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: Then frowned he and showed displeasure.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Then he frowned and he scowled;

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: Then turned his back and waxed proud,

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Then he turned away, and was haughty.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: then he retreated, and waxed proud.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Then turned he back, and grew stiff-necked.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Then he turned back and was proud;

23.author: Itani
23.text: Then he turned back and was proud.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: then he retreated and waxed proud,

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Then he turned back, and was proud.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Then turned he away in pride

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: then he retreated and grew proud

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Then he turned back and was arrogant

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: then turned back, and swelling-up with pride,

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Then he turned back and was big with pride,

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: and he turned his back and behaved arrogantly

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Then he turned back and was haughty;

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: And said: "This is nothing but the magic of old,

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And said, “This is nothing but magic learnt from earlier men.”

24.author: Arberry
24.text: He said, 'This is naught but a trumped-up sorcery;

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Then he said: naught is this but magic from of old;

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: Then he said: "This is nothing but magic from that of old;

24.author: Itani
24.text: And said, “This is nothing but magic from the past.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: and said: “This (Qur'an) is merely a sorcery of yore;

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: Then he said: "This is nothing but magic from that of old,

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: And said: This is naught else than magic from of old;

24.author: Qarai
24.text: saying, ‘It is nothing but traditional sorcery.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: and said: 'This is no more than traced sorcery;

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And said, "This is not but magic imitated [from others].

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: said, "This (the Quran) is nothing but magic, inherited from ancient magicians.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: ~Then he said: This is naught but enchantment, narrated (from others);

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: and said, "This is nothing but sorcery from the ancients.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: Then said he: "This is nothing but magic, derived from of old;

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Faqala in hatha illa sihrun yutharu

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: Nothing more than the speech of a man!"

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: “This is nothing but the speech of a man.”

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: Naught is this but the word of Man.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: "This is nothing but the word of a human being!"

25.author: Itani
25.text: This is nothing but the word of a mortal.”

25.author: Maududi
25.text: this is nothing but the word of a mere mortal!”

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: "This is nothing but the word of a human being!"

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: This is naught else than speech of mortal man.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: It is nothing but the speech of a human.’

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: It is nothing but the word of a mortal'

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: This is not but the word of a human being."

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: These are only words from a mere mortal".

25.author: Shakir
25.text: This is naught but the word of a mortal.

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: This is nothing but the word of a mortal!"

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: "This is nothing but the word of a mortal!"

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: I will cast him into the fire of Hell.

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: I will soon fling him into hell.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: I shall surely roast him in Sakar;

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: Anon shall I roast him into the Scorching Fire.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: I will cast him into Hell-fire

26.author: Itani
26.text: I will roast him in Saqar.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: Him shall I soon roast in Hell.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: I will cast him into Saqar.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: Him shall I fling unto the burning.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: Soon I will cast him into Saqar.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: I will surely roast him in the Scorching.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: I will drive him into Saqar.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: I shall make him suffer the torment of hell.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: I will cast him into hell.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: Soon I will cast him into hell.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: Soon will I cast him into Hell-Fire!

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: What do you think Hell-fire is?

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And what have you understood, what hell is!

27.author: Arberry
27.text: and what will teach thee what is Sakar?

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And what knoweth thou that which the Scorching Fire?

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And what will make you know exactly what Hell-fire is?

27.author: Itani
27.text: But what will explain to you what Saqar is?

27.author: Maududi
27.text: And what do you know what Hell is?

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And what will make you know (exactly) what Saqar is

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: - Ah, what will convey unto thee what that burning is! -

27.author: Qarai
27.text: And what will show you what is Saqar?

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: What will let you know what the Scorching is like!

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And what can make you know what is Saqar?

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: Would that you really knew what hell is!

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And what will make you realize what hell is?

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: What could make you conceive what hell-fire is?

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: And what will explain to thee what Hell-Fire is?

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: It leaves nothing, nor does it spare;

28.author: Arberry
28.text: It spares not, neither leaves alone

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: It shall not spare, nor leave.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: It spares not (any sinner), nor does it leave (anything unburnt)!

28.author: Maududi
28.text: It spares nothing; it leaves nothing intact;

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: It spares not, nor does it leave (anything)!

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: It leaveth naught; it spareth naught

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: It lets nothing remain and leaves nothing [unburned],

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: It leaves and spares no one and nothing.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: It leaves naught nor does it spare aught.

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: It does not allow anyone to live, and neither does it leave anyone to die;

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: Naught doth it permit to endure, and naught doth it leave alone!-

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: It glows and burns the skin.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: It strips away the hide of man.

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: Darkening and changing the colour of man!

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Over it are nineteen (angels as guardians and keepers of Hell).

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: and it has nineteen angelic keepers.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: there are nineteen [angels] in charge of it --

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: We have not appointed any one but angels as keepers of Hell, and their number that We have fixed is to make it a means of contention for disbelievers, so that those who were given the Book may be certain, and the faith of the believers may have greater increase, and the people of the Book and believers may not be deceived, and the sceptics and infidels may say: "What does God mean by this parable?" That is how God leads whosoever He will astray, and guides whosoever He will. None knows the armies of your Lord save Him self. This is no more than reminder for mankind.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: We have not appointed the guards of hell, except angels; and did not keep this number except to test the disbelievers – in order that the People given the Book(s) may be convinced, and to increase the faith of the believers – and so that the People given the Book(s) and the Muslims may not have any doubt – and so that those in whose hearts is a disease and the disbelievers, may say, “What does Allah mean by this amazing example?” This is how Allah sends astray whomever He wills, and guides whomever He wills; and no one knows the armies of Allah except Him; and this is not but an advice to man.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: We have appointed only angels to be masters of the Fire, and their number We have appointed only as a trial for the unbelievers, that those who were given the Book may have certainty, and that those who believe may increase in belief, and that those who were given the Book and those who believe may not be in-doubt, and that those in whose hearts there is sickness, and the unbelievers, may say, 'What did God intend by this as a similitude?' So God leads astray whomsoever He will, and He guides whomsoever He will; and none knows the hosts of thy Lord but He. And it is naught but a Reminder to mortals.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And We have appointed none but the angels to be wardens of the Fire. And their number we have made only a trial for those who disbelieve, so that those who are vouchsafed the Book may be convinced, and that those who believe may increase in faith, and that those who are vouchsafed the Book and the believers may not doubt, and that those in whose hearts is a disease and the infidels may say: what meaneth Allah by this description! In this wise Allah sendeth astray whomsoever He will, and guideth whomsoever He will. And none knoweth the hosts of thy Lord but He. And it is naught but an admonition Unto man.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: And We have set none but angels as guardians of the Fire, and We have fixed their number (19) only as a trial for the disbelievers, in order that the people of the Scripture (Jews and Christians) may arrive at a certainty [that this Quran is the truth as it agrees with their Books i.e. their number (19) is written in the Taurat (Torah) and the Injeel (Gospel)] and the believers may increase in Faith (as this Quran is the truth) and that no doubts may be left for the people of the Scripture and the believers, and that those in whose hearts is a disease (of hypocrisy) and the disbelievers may say: "What Allah intends by this (curious) example?" Thus Allah leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And none can know the hosts of your Lord but He. And this (Hell) is nothing else than a (warning) reminder to mankind.

31.author: Itani
31.text: We have appointed only angels to be wardens of the Fire, and caused their number to be a stumbling block for those who disbelieve; so that those given the Scripture may attain certainty; and those who believe may increase in faith; and those given the Scripture and the believers may not doubt; and those in whose hearts is sickness and the unbelievers may say, “What did God intend by this parable?” Thus God leads astray whom He wills, and guides whom He wills. None knows the soldiers of your Lord except He. This is nothing but a reminder for the mortals.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: We have appointed none but angels as the keepers of the Fire, and We have not made their number but as a trial for the unbelievers so that those who have been endowed with the Book will be convinced and the believers' faith will increase, and neither those who have been endowed with the Book nor the believers will fall into any doubt. As for those in whose hearts there is a sickness as well as the unbelievers, they will say: “What did Allah aim at by this strange parable?” Thus does Allah let whomsoever He pleases to go astray, and directs whomsoever He pleases to the Right Way. And none knows the hosts of your Lord but He. (And Hell has only been mentioned here) that people may take heed.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: And We have set none but angels as guardians of the Fire. And We have fixed their number only as a trial for the disbelievers, in order that the People of the Scripture may arrive at a certainty and that the believers may increase in faith, and that no doubt may be left for the People of the Scripture and the believers, and that those in whose hearts is a disease and the disbelievers may say: "What does Allah intend by this example" Thus Allah leads astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And none can know the hosts of your Lord but He. And this is nothing but a reminder to mankind.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: We have appointed only angels to be wardens of the Fire, and their number have We made to be a stumbling-block for those who disbelieve; that those to whom the Scripture hath been given may have certainty, and that believers may increase in faith; and that those to whom the Scripture hath been given and believers may not doubt; and that those in whose hearts there is disease, and disbelievers, may say: What meaneth Allah by this similitude? Thus Allah sendeth astray whom He will, and whom He will He guideth. None knoweth the hosts of thy Lord save Him. This is naught else than a Reminder unto mortals.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: We have assigned only angels as keepers of the Fire, and We have made their number merely a stumbling block for the faithless, and so that those who were given the Book may be reassured, and the faithful may increase in [their] faith, and so that those who were given the Book and the faithful may not be in doubt, and so that the faithless and those in whose hearts is a sickness may say, ‘What did Allah mean by this description?’ Thus does Allah lead astray whomever He wishes and guides whomever He wishes. No one knows the hosts of your Lord except Him, and it is just an admonition for all humans.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: We have appointed none but angels to guard the Fire, and made their number only as a trial for the unbelievers, so that those to whom the Book was given are certain and those who believe increase in belief. And that those who were given the Book, and those who believe will not be in doubt. And that those in whose hearts there is a sickness, together with the unbelievers, may say: 'What did Allah intend by this as an example' As such, Allah leaves in error whom He will and He guides whomsoever He will. None knows the hosts of your Lord except He. This is no more than a Reminder to humans.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And We have not made the keepers of the Fire except angels. And We have not made their number except as a trial for those who disbelieve - that those who were given the Scripture will be convinced and those who have believed will increase in faith and those who were given the Scripture and the believers will not doubt and that those in whose hearts is hypocrisy and the disbelievers will say, "What does Allah intend by this as an example?" Thus does Allah leave astray whom He wills and guides whom He wills. And none knows the soldiers of your Lord except Him. And mention of the Fire is not but a reminder to humanity.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: We have made only angels as the keepers of the fire (for they are the strongest in carrying out Our commands). Our informing (people) of the numbers of these angels is a trial for the disbelievers. It gives more certainty to the people of the Book and strengthens the faith of the believers. The people of the Book and the believers have no doubt about it. We have fixed the number to make the disbelievers and those whose hearts are sick say, "What does God mean by such a parable?" Thus, God guides and causes to go astray whomever He wants. No one knows about the army of your Lord except He Himself. This parable is a reminder for mankind.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And We have not made the wardens of the fire others than angels, and We have not made their number but as a trial for those who disbelieve, that those who have been given the book may be certain and those who believe may increase in faith, and those who have been given the book and the believers may not doubt, and that those in whose hearts is a disease and the unbelievers may say: What does Allah mean by this parable? Thus does Allah make err whom He pleases, and He guides whom He pleases, and none knows the hosts of your Lord but He Himself; and this is naught but a reminder to the mortals.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: We have appointed only angels to be wardens of the Fire. We have specified their number only as a trial for those who are bent on denying the truth, so that those who were given the Book might gain in certainty, and those who believe might increase in faith -- and so that neither those who have been given the Scripture nor the believers might have any doubts, and that those sick at heart and those who deny the truth might ask, "What does God mean by this parable?" In this way, God lets go astray whom He wills, and guides whom He wills. And none knows the forces of your Lord but He. This is but a Reminder for man.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: And We have set none but angels as Guardians of the Fire; and We have fixed their number only as a trial for Unbelievers,- in order that the People of the Book may arrive at certainty, and the Believers may increase in Faith,- and that no doubts may be left for the People of the Book and the Believers, and that those in whose hearts is a disease and the Unbelievers may say, "What symbol doth Allah intend by this?" Thus doth Allah leave to stray whom He pleaseth, and guide whom He pleaseth: and none can know the forces of thy Lord, except He and this is no other than a warning to mankind.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Wama jaAAalna ashaba alnnari illa malaikatan wama jaAAalna AAiddatahum illa fitnatan lillatheena kafaroo liyastayqina allatheena ootoo alkitaba wayazdada allatheena amanoo eemanan wala yartaba allatheena ootoo alkitaba waalmuminoona waliyaqoola allatheena fee quloobihim maradun waalkafiroona matha arada Allahu bihatha mathalan kathalika yudillu Allahu man yashao wayahdee man yashao wama yaAAlamu junooda rabbika illa huwa wama hiya illa thikra lilbashari

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: I say the truth, and call the moon to witness,

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: Yes, never!* By oath of the moon. (Hell will never spare the disbelievers).

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: By no means! By the moon,

32.author: Shakir
32.text: Nay; I swear by the moon,

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: And the night when on the wane,

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And by oath of the night when it turns back.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: and the night when it retreats

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: By the night when it with draweth.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And by the night when it withdraws,

33.author: Itani
33.text: And the night as it retreats.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: and by the night when it recedes,

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And by the night when it withdraws.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And the night when it withdraweth

33.author: Qarai
33.text: By the night when it recedes!

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And [by] the night when it departs

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And the night when it departs,

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: By the night when it departs.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: And by the Night as it retreateth,

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: The morning when it is unveiled,

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: And by oath of the morning, when it spreads light.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: and the dawn when it is white,

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: By the morning when it brighteneth.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And by the dawn when it brightens,

34.author: Itani
34.text: And the morning as it lights up.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: and by the day when it dawns (with its radiance),

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And by the dawn when it brightens.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: And the dawn when it shineth forth,

34.author: Qarai
34.text: By the dawn when it brightens!

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: and the morning when it appears,

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And [by] the morning when it brightens,

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And the daybreak when it shines;

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: By the dawn when it lightens!

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: And by the Dawn as it shineth forth,-

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: That (Hell) is surely one of the greater (signs),

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: Indeed hell is one of the greatest entities.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: surely it is one of the greatest things

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Verily it is one of the greatest woes.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: Verily, it (Hell, or their denial of the Prophet Muhammad SAW, or the Day of Resurrection) is but one of the greatest calamities.

35.author: Itani
35.text: It is one of the greatest.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: surely (Hell) is one of the greatest Signs,

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: Verily, it is but one of the greatest (signs).

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: Lo! this is one of the greatest (portents)

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Indeed they are one of the greatest [signs of God]

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: it is one of the greatest trials,

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: Indeed, the Fire is of the greatest [afflictions]

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: hell is certainly the greatest calamity.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Surely it (hell) is one of the gravest (misfortunes),

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: Surely, it is one of the gravest things,

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: This is but one of the mighty (portents),

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: It is a warning for mankind

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: it is a warning to man,

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: Whoever of you desires to progress or lag behind.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: For the one among you who wishes to come forward or stay back.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: to whoever of you desires to go forward or lag behind.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: A warning Unto him of you who shall go forward or who chooseth to lag behind.

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: To any of you that chooses to go forward (by working righteous deeds), or to remain behind (by commiting sins),

37.author: Itani
37.text: To whomever among you wishes to advance, or regress.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: a warning to everyone of you whether he would like to come forward or lag behind.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: To any of you that chooses to go forward, or to remain behind.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: Unto him of you who will advance or hang back.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: [alike] for those of you who like to advance ahead and those who would remain behind.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: to whoever among you desires to go forward, or lag behind.

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: To whoever wills among you to proceed or stay behind.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: whether one steps forward to embrace the faith or one turns away from it.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: To him among you who wishes to go forward or remain behind.

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: alike to every one of you, who want to go forward or hang back.

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: To any of you that chooses to press forward, or to follow behind;-

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Liman shaa minkum an yataqaddama aw yataakhkhara

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: Every soul is pledged to what it does,

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: Every soul is mortgaged for its own deeds.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: Every soul shall be pledged for what it has earned,

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: Every soul will be a pledge for that which it hath earned,

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: Every person is a pledge for what he has earned,

38.author: Itani
38.text: Every soul is hostage to what it has earned.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: Each one is a hostage to one's deeds,

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: Every person is a pledge for what he has earned,

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: Every soul is a pledge for its own deeds;

38.author: Qarai
38.text: Every soul is hostage to what it has earned,

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: Each soul is held in pledge for what it earns,

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: Every soul, for what it has earned, will be retained

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: Every soul will be in captivity for its deeds

38.author: Shakir
38.text: Every soul is held in pledge for what it earns,

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: Every soul is held in pledge against its own deeds,

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: Every soul will be (held) in pledge for its deeds.

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: Except those of the right hand

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: Except those on the right side.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: save the Companions of the Right;

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: Save the fellows of the right.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: Except those on the Right, (i.e. the pious true believers of Islamic Monotheism);

39.author: Itani
39.text: Except for those on the Right.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: save the People of the Right Hand

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: Save those who will stand on the right hand.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: except the People of the Right Hand.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: except the Companions of the Right.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: Except the companions of the right,

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: except the people of the right hand

39.author: Shakir
39.text: Except the people of the right hand,

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: except those of the right hand

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: Except the Companions of the Right Hand.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: In Gardens; they shall be asking,

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: In Gardens (Paradise) they will ask one another,

40.author: Maududi
40.text: who shall be in the Gardens, and shall ask

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: In Gardens they will ask one another,

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: In gardens they will ask one another

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: [Who will be] in gardens, questioning each other

40.author: Shakir
40.text: In gardens, they shall ask each other

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: who in their gardens will be enquiring

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: (They will be) in Gardens (of Delight): they will question each other,

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: About Al-Mujrimun (polytheists, criminals, disbelievers, etc.), (And they will say to them):

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: About criminals (and they will say to them):

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: and will ask of the criminals,

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: "What was it that brought you to Hell?"

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: What led you into the Scorching Fire?

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: "What has caused you to enter Hell?"

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: "What has caused you to enter Hell"

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: What hath brought you to this burning?

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: 'What caused you to be thrust into the Scorching (Fire)'

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: [And asking them], "What put you into Saqar?"

42.author: Shakir
42.text: What has brought you into hell?

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: "What has brought you into the Fire of Hell ?"

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: They will answer: "We did not fulfil our devotional obligations,

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: They said, “We never used to offer the prayer.”

43.author: Arberry
43.text: They shall say, 'We were not of those who prayed, and

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: They will say. 'we have not been of those who prayed.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: They will say: "We were not of those who used to offer their Salat (prayers)

43.author: Itani
43.text: They will say, “We were not of those who prayed.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: They will answer: “We were not among those who observed Prayer,

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: They will say: "We were not of those who used to offer the Salah,"

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: They will answer: We were not of those who prayed

43.author: Qarai
43.text: They will answer, ‘We were not among those who prayed.

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: They will reply: 'We were not among those who prayed

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: They will say, "We were not of those who prayed,

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: They will reply, "We did not pray,

43.author: Shakir
43.text: They shall say: We were not of those who prayed;

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: and they shall reply, "We were not among those who prayed;

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: They will say: "We were not of those who prayed;

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: And did not feed the needy,

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: And we have not been feeding the poor.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: "Nor we used to feed Al-Miskin (the poor);

44.author: Itani
44.text: Nor did we feed the destitute.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: and we did not feed the poor,

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Nor did we feed the wretched.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Nor did we feed the poor.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: and we did not feed the needy.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Nor did we used to feed the poor.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: nor did we feed the destitute.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: And we used not to feed the poor;

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: and we did not feed the poor;

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: "Nor were we of those who fed the indigent;

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: And plunged into useless things with those who were obstinate,

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: “And used to dwell on evil matters with those who think evilly.”

45.author: Arberry
45.text: and we plunged along with the plungers,

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: And we have been wading with the waders.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: "And we used to talk falsehood (all that which Allah hated) with vain talkers.

45.author: Itani
45.text: And we used to indulge with those who indulge.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: and we indulged in vain talk with those who indulged in vain talk,

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: "And we used to speak falsehood with vain speakers."

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: We used to wade (in vain dispute) with (all) waders,

45.author: Qarai
45.text: We used to indulge in [profane] gossip along with the gossipers,

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: We plunged in with the plungers

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: And we used to enter into vain discourse with those who engaged [in it],

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: We indulged and persisted in useless disputes,

45.author: Shakir
45.text: And we used to enter into vain discourse with those who entered into vain discourses.

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: we indulged in vain arguments along with those who indulged in them;

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: "But we used to talk vanities with vain talkers;

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: And rejected the Day of Judgement as a lie

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: “And used to deny the Day of Justice.”

46.author: Arberry
46.text: and we cried lies to the Day of Doom,

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: And we have been belying the Day of Requital.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: "And we used to belie the Day of Recompense

46.author: Itani
46.text: And we used to deny the Day of Judgment.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: and we gave the lie to the Day of Judgement

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: "And we used to deny the Day of Recompense,"

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: And we used to deny the Day of Judgment,

46.author: Qarai
46.text: and we used to deny the Day of Retribution

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: and belied the Day of Recompense

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: And we used to deny the Day of Recompense

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: and rejected the Day of Judgment

46.author: Shakir
46.text: And we used to call the day of judgment a lie;

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: and we denied the Day of Judgement

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: "And we used to deny the Day of Judgment,

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: Until the certainty (of death) had come upon us.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: till the Certain came to us.' --

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: until there came to us the certainty.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: "Until there came to us (the death) that is certain."

47.author: Itani
47.text: Until the Inevitable came upon us.”

47.author: Maududi
47.text: until the inevitable event overtook us.”

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: Till the Inevitable came unto us.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: Until there came to us the certainty."

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: until the Inevitable End [death] overtook us."

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: "Until there came to us (the Hour) that is certain."

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: So, the intercession of intercessors will not avail them.

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: So the intercession of the intercessors will not benefit them. (The disbelievers will not have any intercessor.)

48.author: Arberry
48.text: Then the intercession of the intercessors shall not profit them.

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: Then there will not Profit them intercision of the interceders,

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: So no intercession of intercessors will be of any use to them.

48.author: Itani
48.text: But the intercession of intercessors will not help them.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: The intercession of the intercessors shall then be of no avail to them.

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: So no intercession of intercessors will be of any use to them.

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: The mediation of no mediators will avail them then.

48.author: Qarai
48.text: So the intercession of the intercessors will not avail them.

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: The intercession of their intercessors shall not benefit them.

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: So there will not benefit them the intercession of [any] intercessors.

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: The intercession of the intercessors will be of no benefit to them.

48.author: Shakir
48.text: So the intercession of intercessors shall not avail them.

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: So no intercession will avail them.

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: Then will no intercession of (any) intercessors profit them.

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: Why then do they turn away from the admonition

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: So what is the matter with them that they turn away from the advice?

49.author: Arberry
49.text: What ails them, that they turn away from the Reminder,

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: What aileth them, therefore, that they are from the Admonition turning away?

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: Then what is wrong with them (i.e. the disbelievers) that they turn away from (receiving) admonition?

49.author: Itani
49.text: Why are they turning away from the Reminder?

49.author: Maududi
49.text: What is the matter with people that they are turning away from this Exhortation,

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: Then what is wrong with them that they turn away from admonition

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: Why now turn they away from the Admonishment,

49.author: Qarai
49.text: What is the matter with them that they evade the Reminder

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: What is the matter with them that they turn away from this Reminder,

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: Then what is [the matter] with them that they are, from the reminder, turning away

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: If such will be the Day of Judgment, what is the matter with them? Why do they run away from guidance,

49.author: Shakir
49.text: What is then the matter with them, that they turn away from the admonition

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: Then what is wrong with them that they turn away from admonition,

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: Then what is the matter with them that they turn away from admonition?-

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: As though they were frightened asses

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: As if they were startled donkeys –

50.author: Arberry
50.text: as if they were startled asses

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: As though they were asses startled."

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: As if they were frightened (wild) donkeys.

50.author: Itani
50.text: As though they were panicked donkeys.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: as though they were frightened wild asses,

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: As if they were wild donkeys.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: as if they were terrified asses

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: As if they were alarmed donkeys

50.author: Shakir
50.text: As if they were asses taking fright

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: As if they were affrighted asses,

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: Fleeing from a hunter, or a lion, or a beast of prey.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: That had fled from a lion?

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: In fact every one of them wants to be given open books (like the prophets).

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: Rather each one of them desires that he should be given open Books.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: Nay, every man of them desires to be -- given -- scrolls unrolled.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: Aye! every one of them desireth that: he should be vouchsafed scrolls expanded.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: Nay, everyone of them desires that he should be given pages spread out (coming from Allah with a writing that Islam is the right religion, and Muhammad SAW has come with the truth from Allah the Lord of the heavens and earth, etc.).

52.author: Itani
52.text: Yet every one of them desires to be given scrolls unrolled.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: No indeed; each one of them desires that open letters be sent to each of them.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: Nay, everyone of them desires that he should be given pages spread out.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: Nay, but everyone of them desireth that he should be given open pages (from Allah).

52.author: Qarai
52.text: But everyone of them desires to be given unrolled scriptures [from Allah]!

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: Indeed, each one of them desires to be given unrolled scrolls.

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: Rather, every person among them desires that he would be given scriptures spread about.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Is it that everyone of them wants to receive a heavenly book addressed to him personally?

52.author: Shakir
52.text: Nay; every one of them desires that he may be given pages spread out;

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: Indeed, everyone of them desires to have sheets of revelations unfolded before them --

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Forsooth, each one of them wants to be given scrolls (of revelation) spread out!

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Bal yureedu kullu imriin minhum an yuta suhufan munashsharatan

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: By no means. In fact they do not fear the Hereafter.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: Never! In fact they do not fear the Hereafter.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: No indeed; but they do not fear the Hereafter.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: By no means! Aye, they fear not the Hereafter.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: Nay! But they fear not the Hereafter (from Allah's punishment).

53.author: Itani
53.text: No indeed! But they do not fear the Hereafter.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: No indeed; the truth is that they have no fear of the Hereafter.

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: Nay! But they fear not the Hereafter.

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: Nay, verily. They fear not the Hereafter.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: No! Indeed, they do not fear the Hereafter.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: No, indeed they have no fear of the Everlasting Life.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: No! But they do not fear the Hereafter.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: This will certainly never be the case! In fact, they are not afraid of the Day of Judgment.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: Nay! but they do not fear the hereafter.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: No! They do not fear the Hereafter --

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: By no means! But they fear not the Hereafter,

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: Never so, as this is a reminder.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: Yes indeed, this is an advice.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: No indeed; surely it is a Reminder;

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: By no means! Verily this Qur'an is an Admonition.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: Nay, verily, this (Quran) is an admonition,

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: Nay, verily, this is an admonition.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: Nay, verily. Lo! this is an Admonishment.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: No! It is indeed a reminder.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: No, indeed, surely, this is a Reminder.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: No! Indeed, the Qur'an is a reminder

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: There is no doubt that the Quran is a guide.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: Nay! it is surely an admonition.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: but this is truly a reminder.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: Nay, this surely is an admonition:

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: Let him then who heeds remember it.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: So whoever wills may heed advice from it.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: so whoever wills shall remember it.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: So whosoever willeth may heed it.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: So whosoever will (let him read it), and receive admonition (from it)!

55.author: Itani
55.text: So whoever wills, shall remember it.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: So, whoever wills may benefit from it.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: So, whosoever wills receives admonition from it!

55.author: Qarai
55.text: So let anyone who wishes be mindful of it.

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: So, whoever wills, will remember it.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: Then whoever wills will remember it.

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: Let anyone who seeks guidance do so.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: So whoever pleases may mind it.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: Let him who will take heed:

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: Let any who will, keep it in remembrance!

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: But they will not remember except as God wills: He is worthy to be feared, and He is worthy of forgiving

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: And what advice will they heed, except if Allah wills? Only He deserves to be feared, and His only is the greatness of forgiving.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: And they will not remember, except that God wills; He is worthy to be feared, -- worthy to forgive.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: And they shall not heed unless Allah Willeth. He is the Lord of piety. and the Lord of forgiveness

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: And they will not receive admonition unless Allah wills; He (Allah) is the One, deserving that mankind should be afraid of, and should be dutiful to Him, and should not take any Ilah (God) along with Him, and He is the One Who forgives (sins).

56.author: Itani
56.text: But they will not remember, unless God wills. He is the Source of Righteousness, and the Source of Forgiveness.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: But they will not benefit from it unless Allah Himself so wills. He is worthy to be feared; and He is worthy to forgive (those that fear Him).

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: And they will not receive admonition unless Allah wills; He is the One, deserving of the Taqwa and He is the One Who forgives.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: And they will not heed unless Allah willeth (it). He is the fount of fear. He is the fount of Mercy.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: And they will not be mindful unless Allah wishes. He is worthy of [your] being wary [of Him] and He is worthy to forgive.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: But none will remember, unless Allah wills, He is the Owner of fear, the Owner of forgiveness.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: And they will not remember except that Allah wills. He is worthy of fear and adequate for [granting] forgiveness.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: No one will seek guidance unless God wills it. He alone is worthy of being feared and He is the Source of Forgiveness.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: And they will not mind unless Allah please. He is worthy to be feared and worthy to forgive.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: they will only take heed if God so wills: He is the Lord who is worthy to be feared: the Lord of forgiveness.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: But none will keep it in remembrance except as Allah wills: He is the Lord of Righteousness, and the Lord of Forgiveness.

56.author: Transliteration
56.text: Wama yathkuroona illa an yashaa Allahu huwa ahlu alttaqwa waahlu almaghfirati
